title: The Sign of the Cross in Heaven
subtitle: Christ the Judge returns in majesty, the Cross blazing brighter than the sun

stillness: Be still, and let the noise of the world fall away.
If we knew what awaits us, brethren, we would weep without ceasing.
Sit quietly now, and beseech God.
gospel: And then shall appear the sign of the Son of Man in heaven.
And then shall all the tribes of the earth mourn.
And they shall see the Son of Man coming in the clouds of heaven,
with much power and great majesty.
behold: Attend to each thing, as if you were present.
Behold the sign of the Son of Man appearing in the sky:
the sign of the Cross, not wood, but a light shaped like a cross,
more radiant than the sun, blazing brighter than the sun itself.
Behold borne before Him the instruments of His Passion:
the nails, the lance, the crown, the scourges,
carried like the banners of triumph and of victory into judgment.
As when a king enters a city his army goes before him,
lifting high the royal standards to announce the king's coming,
so before the Lord descending from heaven goes the army of Angels and Archangels,
bearing the sign of the Cross as a triumphal banner,
proclaiming to the trembling earth the entrance of the heavenly King.
consider: And then all the tribes of the earth shall mourn,
they who loved the world more than Christ.
They shall bewail themselves: their guilt, which they committed;
their folly, which they did not correct;
their glory, which they lost;
their punishment, which they incurred.
For they shall hear Him say these words to them:
I was made man for you; for you I was bound, mocked, spat upon, beaten, crucified.
Where then is the fruit of so many of My injuries?
Behold the price of My blood, which I gave for the redemption of your souls.
I held you above My own glory, appearing as a man though I was God,
and you made Me cheaper than all your possessions.
Every vilest thing of earth you loved more than My justice and My faith.
Justly shall they mourn:
for there is no power to resist Him, no means to flee before His face, no place left for repentance.
Out of the anguish of all things, nothing remains for them but wailing and grief.
Money does not avail the rich for almsgiving;
righteous parents do not intercede for their children, nor the Angels for men,
for the nature of judgment does not receive mercy, as the time of mercy did not receive judgment.
Mercy in the first coming; judgment in the second.
Tell me, He will say, you sinners passible and mortal in the flesh,
what have you suffered for Me, your Lord, who though I cannot suffer, suffered for you?
His first coming was a showing of weakness; His second will be a showing of power and majesty.
He whom they would not hear set down in lowliness, they shall see in power and majesty.
Then shall the whole Christ, with His members, appear in glory, clothed in light as with a garment.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, who are to come to judge with power and great majesty,
while the Angels bear before You the sign of the glorious Cross as a triumphal banner,
seal my soul with the imprint of this most holy Cross;
and through it claim me wholly for Yourself,
so that the adversary may find nothing in me;
that when You come to judgment, I may be found marked with this venerable sign,
and, conformed to You crucified in suffering,
may merit to become a sharer also in You risen in glory. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Crux apparebit sole luculentior existens, ut non sit necessitas accusationis cum viderint crucem.
latinJewel.english: The Cross shall appear, shining brighter than the sun, so that no accusation will be needed once they have seen the Cross.
